title: How automated are operator networks today – really?
summary: As telcos boast of L4 autonomy and agentic AI at FutureNet World, Fierce Network checked in with Blue Planet and Netcracker, who both cautioned that hype often runs far ahead of operational reality.

title: Deutsche Telekom eyes full combo with T-Mobile US
summary: Is Germany’s Deutsche Telekom (DT) considering a full combination with T-Mobile? That’s what Bloomberg is reporting, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Is the telco arms race finally over? It is for Vodafone
summary: After years of talking about digital transformation, the language has shifted. Telcos now talk about evolving their operating structures. For Vodafone, that means renting GPUs instead of buying.

title: Rakuten Symphony and Celona team on open RAN for private 5G
summary: The collaboration will marry Rakuten Symphony’s cloud-native 5G open RAN infrastructure with Celona’s 5G LAN enterprise platform.

title: Learner’s permits are the secret to AT&T’s internal AI rollout
summary: AT&T is one of the leaders in telco AI adoption. Exec BJ Nab shared its GenAI playbook and how its "permit to learn" approach is preparing employees for the agentic wave.

title: C Spire puts AI to work in its network operations center
summary: The regional U.S. operator teamed with AWS to give its NOC team an agentic AI boost. C Spire Chief Network Officer Alan Jones gave Fierce and inside look at how it built the system.

title: Charter loses 120,000 broadband subs in Q1 2026
summary: Charter CFO Jessica Fischer said the company expects run-rate, operating expense synergies after its Cox acquisition of at least $800 million.

title: SpaceX doesn’t always get what it wants at the FCC
summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) on Thursday denied requests by multiple satellite companies – including SpaceX – for access to spectrum, saying it’s all about enabling the direct-to-device (D2D) market to flourish.

title: Comcast’s Q1 2026 broadband losses were ‘less bad’ than expected
summary: One analyst said, “A single quarter of ‘less bad’ results isn’t reason to declare victory. But turnarounds must start somewhere. It’s not unreasonable to be at least a little optimistic.”

title: Nokia: Machine AI will drive most network traffic
summary: Machine-to-machine AI traffic will overtake human use and reshape networks for years, Nokia CEO Justin Hotard said on the Q1 earnings call.

title: Verizon is eerily quiet. What's up with that?
summary: Verizon's in turn-around mode – and hunkered down.

title: Opinion: US superintelligence is dumb
summary: ML, LLMs and chatbots - the sum of these parts does not a superintelligence make. Best case, it leads to a very expensive, very power-hungry Magic Eight Ball the size of a small moon.

title: Tower co Vertical Bridge gets $1.5B boost from KKR
summary: “This transaction provides us with the resources to continue developing our portfolio at scale," Vertical Bridge CEO Ron Bizick said.

title: Podcast: Data Center 2.0—Decoding modern data storage
summary: The data center isn't just about GPUs. There is another side of the equation that decides whether that expensive compute power actually does anything useful: Storage. In this episode,  we tackle what storage is, what decisions make the biggest impact, key challenges and how to handle the current storage shortage.

title: Comcast’s Xfinity Mobile launches new price plans
summary: Comcast unveiled two new Xfinity Mobile price plans, but they come with a somewhat surprising twist.

title: Zayo’s long-haul business is going gangbusters because of AI
summary: The company recently signed a new “monster” contract with an AI infrastructure partner as an anchor tenant.

title: AT&T CEO keeps LEO options open
summary: Convergence was once again a strong theme during AT&T's Q1 earnings call, but CEO John Stankey also talked about the D2D market and perceived threats.

title: Render acquires mPower as telecom, electric infra converge
summary: Render is already known for its telco project planning software but is adding new capabilities for electrical projects as well. CEO Stephen Rose said the move reflects the reality of where the market is headed.

title: Opinion: Where will AI inference happen?
summary: Today, AI inference is done in two places: either on a mobile device or in a centralized data center. Not much in between. But that is going to change.

title: VAST Data raised another $1B. Here’s where its CEO says the money’s going
summary: “What’s become apparent is that the old stack is not good enough for these new AI applications and models,” VAST Data CEO Renen Hallak told Fierce. So it's building a new one.

title: Telesat works with Canada’s government to reach remote areas with LEO
summary: A Telesat executive told Fierce the company is "a carrier's carrier." Its satellite network acts as an extension of a telco’s Layer 2, Carrier Ethernet. "We've created basically a telco in space.”

title: Rakuten Mobile’s journey to autonomy traces back to 26‑year‑old academic research
summary: Rakuten Mobile is beginning to see what full network autonomy looks like — starting with energy savings — thanks in part to early research by Petrit Nahi, senior member of the technical staff, CTO Office.

title: Layoff tracker — Meta, Microsoft eliminate 20,000-plus jobs
summary: Tech and telecom companies are facing new pressures to pare down their workforces. Here's our rundown of the most consequential layoffs in 2026.

title: Career moves — Cook steps aside, John Ternus will lead Apple
summary: Welcome to Career Moves, Fierce's weekly rundown of recent hirings, firings and retirings across the tech and telecom sectors.

title: 2026 M&A tracker — TDS Telecom and GCI acquire small providers
summary: It's increasingly difficult to stay on top of the ever-changing business landscape the underpins the telecom and tech industries. We're here to help!
